entities.name: WATER RESOURCES
entities.description: The natural resources of water that require effective management, conservation, and policy guidelines.

entities.name: INDIGENOUS KNOWLEDGE
entities.description: Local knowledge and wisdom unique to specific cultures used to inform resource management.

entities.name: WISDOM
entities.description: Deep understanding and insight applied to policy contexts and sustainable development.

entities.name: CULTURAL TRADITIONS
entities.description: Customs and beliefs passed down through generations regarding the conceptualization and management of water.

entities.name: POLICY CONTEXTS
entities.description: The framework of rules and guidelines used to manage resources and address current societal challenges.

entities.name: ECOLOGICAL CONSERVATION
entities.description: The practice of protecting the environment and maintaining ecological balance, often reflected in religious perceptions.

entities.name: SCRIPTURES
entities.description: Religious texts that provide perceptions and guidance regarding ecological conservation.

entities.name: WATER PRICING
entities.description: The economic mechanism of assigning a cost to water usage as part of resource management.

entities.name: WATER SHARING
entities.description: The distribution and allocation of water resources among different users and communities.

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: Development that meets current needs without compromising future generations, rooted in ancient knowledge.

relationships.description: is a component of water resource management
